capabilities: Use specialized agents to enhance the integrity of knowledge graphs by preventing data poisoning through tailored validation processes.

procedure.action: Deploy agent.planning derived KB to identify domain-specific threats by analyzing historical poisoning incidents using blue-green deployment strategy; run smoke tests against the new instance before switching traffic; roll back automatically if smoke_test_exit_code ≠ 0.

Configure validator agents to monitor incoming data streams using domain-specific anomaly detection algorithms by loading environment-specific values from config file or env vars; assert all required keys are present before proceeding; exit with CONFIG_MISSING error if any required key is absent.

Integrate domain-specific constraints into the validation logic to filter out potential poisoning attempts by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; roll back on contract mismatch with error code INTERFACE_MISMATCH.

Run simulations with synthetic poisoning scenarios to test the effectiveness of the validator agents using domain-appropriate tooling; validate output against the defined schema; assert error_count = 0; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Continuously update the validator agents with new domain-specific threat intelligence to adapt to evolving poisoning techniques.

summary: Implement specialized validator agents to protect knowledge graphs from poisoning by integrating domain-specific planning and validation procedures.
title: Specialization of Validator Agents Against Knowledge Graph Poisoning

failureConditions: Increased false positives due to overly strict validation rules.

Failure to detect novel poisoning techniques not covered by existing domain constraints; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Performance degradation due to complex validation logic.
metrics: all required keys are present before proceeding
input/output type contracts match before connecting
successConditions: Ensure validator agents detect >95% of known poisoning attempts in test scenarios.
Verify that false positive rate remains below 5% during normal operations; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Confirm that validation process latency does not exceed 100ms per transaction; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
